mechanism_narrative: Persistent high federal funds rates, as currently observed, indicate a restrictive monetary policy environment. This typically increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate from its current low level of 3.7% signals a deteriorating labor market. This reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon of 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months from its current level of 3.7%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat (average return between -0.1% and 0.1%) following a 0.5pp increase in UNRATE over 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is robust (INDPRO > 100) but retail sales growth decelerates significantly, it suggests an inventory build-up and weakening consumer demand. This mismatch negatively impacts future corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 100 and RSAFS 3-month average growth rate falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive (average return > 0.2%) when industrial production is strong but retail sales growth is weak, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts, currently at 1401.0, indicate robust economic activity and consumer confidence. This typically translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns, especially during periods of economic recovery, over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative more than 55% of the time when HOUST is above 1200 during a recovery regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins. This leads to tighter monetary policy and negative sentiment for equity markets, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive (average return > 0.3%) during periods of sustained high inflation (CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3%), this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong non-farm payroll employment growth signals a healthy and expanding labor market. This supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time when PAYEMS 3-month average growth rate exceeds 0.1%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth indicates a strong overall economy, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence. This drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative (average return < -0.1%) during periods of sustained real GDP growth above 2% annualized, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden, unexpected cut in the federal funds rate by the central bank signals an attempt to stimulate the economy. This can boost market sentiment and lead to short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as discount rates adjust.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative (average return < -0.1%) following an unexpected FEDFUNDS rate cut of 0.25 percentage points or more, this hypothesis is rejected.
